known_risk_notes: Dependency on market-data-gateway is a single point of failure for real-time pricing. No read-replica or circuit-breaker pattern is currently implemented. Privileged access to portfolio processing nodes is controlled through iam-core PAM integration.

statement-renderer-saas is a third-party SaaS vendor with no contractual failover SLA. Disk saturation on rep-db-01 has caused backup failures historically. Service account svc-reporting has broad read access to client data and is a high-value target. Output staging on document-store is not replicated — a storage failure during rendering would require full reprocessing.

NAV calculation failure has direct regulatory consequences for fund reporting deadlines. pricing-feed-hub has experienced two latency incidents in the past 12 months. queue-broker is a shared resource — contention from other workloads can delay batch job distribution. Worker restart storms under memory pressure have been the most frequent failure mode.

iam-core is a shared dependency across the entire production estate. Its failure cascades into authentication failures for all services that depend on sso-gateway. Break-glass account usage bypasses normal authorisation controls — each use must be individually reviewed. PAM vault rotation failures have previously left break-glass credentials stale for extended windows without detection.

Real-time order flow; any outage during market hours triggers MiFID II incident clock. Vendor dependency on trade-execution-gateway is single-point; no fallback path approved. trade-admin account has direct database access — a credential compromise would allow access to pre-publication order flow.

Feeds regulatory submissions. Degraded external-data-feed vendor causes stale data in EMIR reports. compliance-admin account has broad read access to regulatory data — privileged access anomalies on this account carry elevated risk. Repeated failed logins flagged in last audit cycle.
